authoritative_sources.title: GSA CBS Submittal Matrix (2025) - Version 1.0
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is a major public owner and this matrix is authoritative for architecture and engineering phase deliverables, milestone review packages, and discipline-specific submission evidence.
used_for: scenario design for incomplete phase packages

authoritative_sources.title: GSA BIM Guide Series 01: Overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's overview guide is an authoritative owner reference for BIM use cases, project phases, owner value framing, and performance-oriented BIM deployment in architecture and engineering projects.
used_for: metric selection boundaries for BIM-enabled projects

authoritative_sources.title: GSA BIM Guide 07 - Building Elements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA BIM Guide 07 is authoritative for building-element information, LOD progression, naming conventions, IFC coordination, model checking, clash detection, and BEP content by project phase.

authoritative_sources.title: A/E/C Computer-Aided Design (CAD) Standard : Release 6.2
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Army Engineer Research and Development Center / U.S. Army Corps of Engineers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE publishes a public A/E/C CAD standard used across federal built-environment work, making it authoritative for digital file naming, layer assignments, symbology, and drawing-package evidence controls.

authoritative_sources.title: National BIM Guide for Owners
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIBS is a recognized U.S. built-environment standards body, and this guide is a direct workflow reference for owner BIM requirements, contract framing, process, infrastructure, standards, and execution expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Project BIM Execution Planning (BEP) Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NBIMS-US BEP Standard is a recognized public standard for owner, proposal, and project BEPs, information requirements, BIM uses, quality management, and information-management risk handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: BIM execution planning and information-exchange setup

authoritative_sources.title: Construction to Operations Building Information Exchange (COBie) V3
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: COBie is the recognized public handover-data standard within NBIMS-US and is authoritative for milestone-based asset-data delivery, table structures, required fields, and operations-handover evidence.
used_for: handover completeness and field-level control checks

authoritative_sources.title: Model Rules (Revised August 2022)
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Examiners for Engineering and Surveying
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCEES is the national engineering and surveying licensure body for U.S. member boards, and its model rules are authoritative for responsible charge, seal-on-documents posture, competence, and truthful professional statements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Licensed engineering boundary and disclosure review

authoritative_sources.title: Model Rules of Conduct
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Architectural Registration Boards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCARB is the national architecture licensure council for U.S. member boards, and its model rules are authoritative for competence, full disclosure, law compliance, and signing-and-sealing boundaries for architects.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Architectural signoff and public-statement boundary review
used_for: architect competence and public disclosure limits

authoritative_sources.title: Commission Guidance on Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is the authoritative U.S. public baseline for explaining why a metric is useful, how it is calculated, and when changes in methodology or assumptions must be disclosed before metrics are published externally.

workflow_stages.name: Source qualification and project-context setup
workflow_stages.description: Identify project type, client, contract form, delivery method, phase, discipline ownership, jurisdiction, and reporting purpose before any metric is selected.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric-definition and artifact-lineage mapping
workflow_stages.description: Determine which utilization, fee, phase-gate, drawing, model, issue, permit, and handover definitions apply and map each metric to named architecture and engineering artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-system reconciliation and evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Compare PSA or ERP, timekeeping, CDE, BIM, CAD, QA or QC, permit, and reporting records to identify matched metrics, unexplained variances, unsupported readiness states, and source-of-truth conflicts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis and disclosure package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble analytical briefs, dashboards, or disclosure-oriented summaries with explicit caveats, source lineage, refresh assumptions, phase context, and methodology labeling.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when work requires sign-and-seal authority, code-sensitive judgment, permit approval, client contract interpretation, scope or fee approval, or public disclosure approval.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: project charters, contracts, delivery-method definitions, phase schedules, and milestone maps
timesheets, utilization logs, WIP reports, fee burn summaries, earned-hours workpapers, and PSA or ERP extracts
submittal matrices, transmittals, drawing and sheet registers, revision logs, and title-block metadata
BEPs, project BIM requirements, model progression matrices, federated-model snapshots, clash reports, and model-check outputs
design-review comments, redline logs, QA or QC checklists, interdisciplinary issue registers, and decision logs
permit-set packages, agency comments, resubmission trackers, code-review notes, and seal or discipline-responsibility matrices
RFI, bulletin, ASI, CCD, and change logs plus downstream impact notes
COBie exports, handover lists, asset-data packages, closeout notes, BI extracts, and disclosure review notes
systems_of_record: project or practice-management PSA or ERP system
common data environment or document-control platform
clash detection, issue tracking, and QA or QC review systems
data warehouse, BI, and reporting repository

allowed: Define architecture-and-engineering metrics and source hierarchies when project phase, discipline, jurisdiction, and reporting purpose are explicit.
Assemble evidence-backed analytical briefs, caveats, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk summaries.
Separate fee, utilization, phase-gate, model, drawing, permit, and handover record paths when the public baseline requires different evidence or definitions.
escalate: Client contract terms, jurisdictional instructions, permit posture, or phase-gate definitions are missing or conflict with the public baseline.
PSA or ERP, timekeeping, CDE, BIM, CAD, QA or QC, and permit records materially disagree on a publishable metric.
A requested claim requires architect-of-record or engineer-of-record judgment, code interpretation, permit approval, scope or fee approval, or public-disclosure approval.
refuse: Requests to sign or seal technical submissions, certify code compliance, or treat permit readiness as approved.
Requests to treat phase-complete, owner-reviewed, coordinated, submit-ready, sealed, and handover-complete as interchangeable without mapped definitions.
Requests that use the architecture-and-engineering industry label as cosmetic and skip phase, discipline, BIM, CAD, review, permit, or licensure context.

domain_failure_modes: A portfolio metric is treated as comparable even though projects use different delivery methods, phase gates, fee structures, and contractual deliverables.
A dashboard treats a single warehouse extract as the source of truth even when PSA or ERP, CDE, BIM, CAD, QA or QC, and permit systems disagree.
Internal model or drawing issue closure is treated as coordinated, permit-ready, or seal-ready even though design-review, jurisdictional, or licensure evidence is unresolved.
Unsupported causal, benchmark, or forecast language is published because a stakeholder wants a cleaner performance story than the evidence supports.
The lane drifts into project delivery approval, design quality signoff, permitting authority, or licensed architect or engineer judgment.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific digital seal, responsible-control, and permit-submission rules vary materially across states and agencies.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: A metric or readiness claim can overstate confidence if local licensure and submission rules are assumed rather than retrieved.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_runtime_handling: Require jurisdiction, discipline-of-record mapping, local seal rules, and permit-path ownership before choosing readiness or signoff-sensitive language.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant firms use different fee-burn, utilization, earned-hours, and phase-completion definitions depending on contract type and delivery method.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Cross-project comparisons and trend claims can be misleading if tenant metric dictionaries and contract-specific overrides are not retrieved.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_runtime_handling: Require tenant metric dictionary, contract type, delivery method, phase-gate map, and source precedence before selecting denominator logic or comparability language.

notes: The research set covers official owner deliverable guidance, BIM and CAD workflow standards, handover information-exchange standards, licensure boundaries, and KPI disclosure discipline tied directly to architecture and engineering analytics.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because every identified ambiguity has an explicit runtime retrieval or escalation rule.
